timeline_html:
Summary. Jennifer experienced a significant illness in early 2024 followed by a long stretch of major emotional stress that lasted until late 2025. She began noticing extra shedding around early 2026, which has since steadied.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Major illness with fever (resolved; Jan 2024–Jan 2024)
  • Major emotional stress (resolved; Jul 2024–Nov 2025)

Timeline forecast. Based on the timing, shedding likely peaked around September–October 2025, with the calm phase beginning in early 2026. Stabilization was expected by mid‑2026, and gentle regrowth should begin toward late 2026 as follicles recover. Dominant driver: Emotional stress, Jul 2024–Nov 2025, as it overlapped the longest and aligns with the delayed onset of shedding.

Where you are now. You’re in the stabilization phase — shedding has leveled off, but baby hairs aren’t visible yet. This is often when the scalp quietly resets before new sprouts appear, so patience will pay off soon. 🌱
